response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with genuine developer-tooling depth — test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD infrastructure, Selenium training courses, and recent AI/MCP dev-tooling work — plus some mobile experience (iOS/Apple TV). However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring at least 2 years of people management and 3 years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of formal people management, performance reviews, one-on-ones, or organizational change leadership. The trajectory is consistently IC-focused, making the fit poor for an Engineering Manager position despite the relevant technical domain.
strengths: Deep developer-tools and testing-infrastructure background (test runners, assertion libraries, CI servers, device orchestration tooling)
18+ years of software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Teaching and community leadership (workshops, Selenium courses, code katas) suggests mentoring aptitude
concerns: No formal people-management experience — a hard minimum requirement (2+ years) for this role
No demonstrated experience setting team priorities, roadmaps, or leading organizational change
Mobile-platform exposure is dated (2015-2016) and limited relative to the role's mobile-ecosystem focus
